title: When MAGA trumped FIFA — Football caves to Trump tantrum, revokes US striker Balogun's suspension
content: Donald Trump contacted FIFA President Gianni Infantino regarding a red card suspension.  FIFA then invoked an obscure rule to freeze the player's one-match ban.  This action has caused significant controversy within the global football community.  Critics argue that political interference should not influence sporting regulations.  The incident highlights broader geopolitical and immigration themes connected to the player.

title: Ram Mandir trust opens its vaults: Rs 3,264 crore spent, silver donations melted - details
content: The Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust detailed its finances and accepted key resignations.  Over Rs 3,264 crore was received, with Rs 2,370 crore spent on temple construction.  Devotees can verify their donations by visiting Ayodhya and inspecting records.  Silver donations were melted into bars, with records available for inspection.  The Trust is implementing reforms to enhance transparency and efficiency in its operations.

title: What Trump said to FIFA chief about Folarin Balogun's red card - Full conversation
content: US President Donald Trump confirmed his call to FIFA president Gianni Infantino.  He asked Infantino to review Folarin Balogun's red card and suspension.  FIFA later lifted the suspension, allowing Balogun to play.  This decision sparked praise from Trump and outrage from Belgium.  The suspension reversal was an extraordinary move in World Cup history.

title: Microsoft layoffs: Company cuts 4,800 jobs; to reduce 2.1% global workforce
content: Microsoft has laid off 4,800 employees, about 2.1% of its global workforce, as its new fiscal year begins, with Xbox and commercial sales taking the biggest hits. HR chief Amy Coleman told employees the business is changing because the industry around it is changing, insisting AI isn't replacing these roles. Xbox alone loses 1,600 jobs now, with 3,200 more planned this year as CEO Asha Sharma resets the gaming division.

title: Weeks before Alexander Wang’s AI update tease, Google ‘messaged’ Meta
content: Google capped Meta's use of Gemini AI models around March 2026 after Meta sought more computing capacity than Google could provide. The restriction disrupted Meta's internal AI projects and forced employees to ration AI tokens. It came weeks before Meta AI chief Alexandr Wang announced a Muse Spark update aimed at cutting the company's dependence on rival models for coding and agentic tasks.

title: How much did Khamenei's funeral cost? State spending draws scrutiny amid economic hardship
content: Iranians criticize the state funeral for Ali Khamenei due to extensive public spending.  This expenditure contrasts sharply with the economic hardships faced by ordinary citizens.  Critics allege authorities used state employees and free services to inflate funeral attendance figures.  Such actions highlight a perceived disconnect between the government and the general populace.  The funeral costs remain unconfirmed, with some estimates reaching $800 million.

title: When Haaland played against India: Norway star's FIFA WC heroics revive old memory
content: Haaland scored twice as Norway beat Brazil 2-1 to reach the World Cup quarterfinals for the first time. The striker headed in the go-ahead goal in the 79th minute before adding another before the end of regulation time to send his team through.

title: 'Was alive when thrown into pond': Bengal girl's autopsy reveals bite marks across body
content: A horrific crime has emerged in West Bengal's Baruipur, where an 11-year-old girl, allegedly raped and murdered, was found to have been thrown into a pond alive. Preliminary post-mortem reports indicate severe injuries and water in her lungs, suggesting drowning as a cause of death following head trauma. Police are analyzing CCTV footage and have arrested a prime suspect, with investigations ongoing.

title: Jamie Dimon gives a 'reality check' to Canada's PM Mark Carney on his unity call to Europe
content: JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon has dismissed Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney's call for middle powers to unite, calling it a "fantasy." Dimon pointed to Europe's economic struggles as an example of cooperation's limitations, citing high taxes and regulation. He emphasized that attractive business environments are crucial for growth, contrasting Europe's declining competitiveness with the U.S. market. Dimon also reassured investors about America's investment appeal, noting China's evolving global approach.

title: 'There can never be justice': Punjab 'fake' encounter victims' kin seek 'Satluj' release
content: Families impacted by enforced disappearances and staged encounters during the tumultuous years of Punjab’s militancy are passionately advocating for the screening of the controversial film 'Satluj'. They assert that the film sheds light on a vital yet suppressed segment of their history. Many recounted harrowing tales of loved ones taken by police and never seen again, with news of deaths often emerging from informal sources.

title: 32-year-old spends 43 days alone at sea rowing from California to Hawaii
content: Kelsey Pfendler, an intrepid American adventurer, has made history by becoming the first American woman to undertake a solo rowing expedition from California to Hawaii. She accomplished the remarkable 2,400-mile journey in less than 44 days, breaking existing records. Her incredible adventure, shared online, showcased her tenacity in facing tough conditions and raised over $30,000 for the Whale Foundation, earning her a warm welcome in Honolulu.

title: SpaceX burned up 260 Starlink satellites in six months, with hundreds more waiting
content: SpaceX is planning to intentionally de-orbit numerous Starlink satellites, incinerating them in Earth's atmosphere from late 2025 to mid-2026. This controlled disposal method is essential for maintaining their expansive satellite fleet and preventing any debris from reaching the surface. Although SpaceX assures complete destruction of the satellites, scientists express concerns regarding the potential long-lasting effects these materials may have on the upper atmosphere.

title: Sergey Brin leads tech elite's millions against California billionaire tax
content: California's proposed 5% wealth tax on billionaires is facing a massive opposition campaign funded by tech titans like Sergey Brin, who has donated $82 million.  Wealthy individuals are reportedly moving assets out of state, while supporters argue the tax will fund vital public services and reduce inequality. This high-stakes battle, with millions being spent on both sides, will be decided by voters this November.

title: 145% rise in LPG imports: Gas buys to be doubled from US — how much can it help India?
content: Energy imports from the US have gained importance, particularly following the West Asia conflict, helping reduce the trade imbalance between the two countries. Besides the US, India has also sourced LPG from Argentina, Nigeria and Malaysia. Data from Kpler shows that India’s LPG imports from America have grown substantially since the US-Iran war began. After a marginal dip in April, the supplies have steadily climbed.

title: 'Made right decision': Trump confirms he asked FIFA to review Balogun decision
content: US President Donald Trump said he personally asked FIFA president Gianni Infantino to review the red card and one-match suspension handed to Team USA striker Folarin Balogun, a move that has sparked major controversy at the FIFA World Cup, according to CNN.

title: Allu Arjun virtual court appearance: Actor named accused in stampede case
content: Actor Allu Arjun virtually appeared before a Hyderabad court on July 6, 2026, regarding the fatal Sandhya Theatre stampede during a 'Pushpa: The Rule' screening. The incident tragically claimed the life of Revathi and injured her son. Arjun is named Accused No. 11, with theatre management as Accused 1-10. The court adjourned the case to July 29.

title: Blake Lively, Ryan Reynolds reportedly devastated over Swift-Kelce wedding snub
content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce just had the wedding of the year as billions across the world cheered them on throughout the world across countries. While the wedding pictures have been kept strictly private, Blake Lively and Ryan Reynolds remained missing from the party. At a time when Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce publicly distanced themselves from the Hollywood power couple, it seems both Blake Lively and Ryan Reynolds are furious at this.

title: Who is Erling Haaland's sister? Gabrielle goes viral at FIFA World Cup 2026
content: Erling Haaland has become one of the biggest stories of the FIFA World Cup 2026. While the Norway striker has impressed on the pitch, one of his family members has unexpectedly grabbed attention off it. His older sister, Gabrielle Braut Haaland, has gone viral after cameras spotted her cheering from the stands.

title: Canadiens eye trade involving $4M forward ahead of 2026-27 NHL season
content: The Montreal Canadiens are said to be considering trade options for forward Kirby Dach, as the organization continues its search for a reliable second-line center. There’s no deal on the horizon, but talks suggest the franchise is considering several roster options as it heads into the 2026-27 NHL season. Those conversations still revolve around Dach’s contract status, injury history and untapped potential.

title: 'He is untainted in my eyes': Ram temple trust treasurer defends Champat Rai after resignation
content: Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust Treasurer Govind Dev Giri defended former general secretary Champat Rai following his resignation over a donation theft case, calling him "untainted" and his decision voluntary. The trust accepted resignations of Rai and trustee Anil Mishra after a preliminary report. Krishna Mohan takes over as interim general secretary, and a committee will recommend a permanent replacement.

title: Sanju Samson out of India squad; Ashok Sharma earns maiden call-up for Zimbabwe tour
content: Sanju Samson misses out on India's T20I squad for Zimbabwe due to a slump in form, having struggled in recent outings. Shreyas Iyer will lead the 15-member team, which includes a maiden call-up for pacer Ashok Sharma and retains young Vaibhav Sooryavanshi. Meanwhile, Shivam Dube has been added to the ODI squad as a replacement.

title: Saudi Arabia slashes August oil prices for Asia by $11 in biggest cut in over 20 years
content: Saudi Arabia has slashed its crude oil prices for Asian customers by a significant $11 per barrel for August, the largest reduction in over two decades. This move reflects weakening demand in Asia and easing geopolitical tensions, which have stabilized global oil markets. With supply conditions improving and Middle Eastern shipping routes normalizing, Saudi Aramco is adjusting its strategy to maintain market share amidst increased competition.

title: TN SSLC revaluation result 2026 released: Check direct link to download scorecards
content: Tamil Nadu's Directorate of Government Examinations has released SSLC revaluation and retotalling results for 2026. Students who applied can now check their updated Class 10 scores on the official website by entering their roll number and date of birth. The board confirmed that revised marks are final for all academic purposes.  Those not listed have no mark changes. Contact helpline for queries.

title: Why FIFA just can't say no to Donald Trump
content: The 2026 World Cup was always going to be too big to remain just football. It is the first 48-team edition, spread across the US, Mexico and Canada, stretched over 104 matches and sold as the biggest, grandest and most lucrative tournament FIFA has ever staged. It is also being played in the year America marks its 250th anniversary, which gives Donald Trump exactly the kind of backdrop he understands instinctively: flags, stadiums, anthems, cameras, national theatre and the possibility of a home team carrying the pageant deeper into the month.

title: Ram temple trust gets interim general secretary after Champat Rai's exit, who is Krishna Mohan
content: Krishna Mohan has taken over as interim general secretary of the Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust, replacing Champat Rai who resigned citing moral responsibility amidst donation embezzlement allegations. Trustee Anil Mishra also stepped down. The Uttar Pradesh government has ordered a probe, and an FIR has been registered with several arrests made. The Trust emphasizes thorough investigation and administrative reforms for enhanced transparency.

title: NHAI project director arrested: CBI alleges Rs 12 lakh bribe demand to clear contractor's bill
content: The CBI has apprehended the NHAI Project Director in Shillong, Aanand Singh Chouhan, along with two private individuals, for allegedly demanding a Rs 12 lakh bribe. The bribe was sought to clear a contractor's pending payment of Rs 13.38 crore.  An initial Rs 4 lakh advance was caught red-handed during a sting operation, leading to the arrests and ongoing investigations.

title: Court grants bail to woman accused in TCS Nashik sexual harassment, conversion case
content: A Nashik court granted bail to a woman accused in a sexual harassment and religious conversion case at TCS, citing her pregnancy. However, her co-accused was denied bail. The prosecution alleged evidence of sexual assault and attempts to convert the victim, a Scheduled Caste employee, to Islam. A Special Investigation Team is probing multiple related cases at the TCS unit.

title: Crude oil relief: OMCs may break even on petrol, diesel rates soon; what it means
content: Public sector refiners have collectively absorbed losses of over Rs 75,000 crore during the first quarter of the current financial year. Since they continue to process higher-cost crude purchased earlier, profitability is expected to remain under pressure during the ongoing quarter.

title: Twist in Balogun row: Belgium allowed to appeal against FIFA decision; what it means
content: Balogun had been due to miss the United States' FIFA World Cup round-of-16 match against Belgium after receiving a direct red card against Bosnia and Herzegovina, a dismissal that normally carries an automatic one-game suspension. However, FIFA announced on Sunday that "the one-game ban had been suspended," making the striker available for selection.

title: Potholes, now landslide: Rs 6,695cr Mumbai-Pune expressway link could not survive one monsoon
content: Heavy rains triggered a landslide near the Missing Link tunnel, forcing the closure of the Mumbai-Pune Expressway and old highway on Monday. This disruption, barely nine weeks after its inauguration, also impacted rail traffic, leading to train cancellations and diversions. The incident follows earlier concerns about the new stretch's road quality, highlighting ongoing challenges with the ambitious infrastructure project.

title: Explained: Indian Navy’s new sonar plan to counter China-Pakistan underwater threat
content: Facing growing underwater threats from China and Pakistan, the Indian Navy is bolstering its anti-submarine capabilities. A new containerized sonar system, the CTLS, is being developed for rapid deployment and enhanced detection. This advanced system will utilize both active and passive listening modes, incorporating AI for improved accuracy. Additionally, the Navy is acquiring more P-8I maritime patrol aircraft, reinforcing its underwater surveillance network.

title: Unlike Ponting or Kallis: Why Root has a genuine shot at Tendulkar's 15,921
content: Joe Root now stands second on the all-time Test run charts with 14,114 runs, leaving him 1,807 short of Sachin Tendulkar's record. Plenty of batters have climbed into the conversation over the last decade before fading away. The difference this time isn't just the gap. It's where Root is in his career.

title: China is building a different kind of AI beyond classic chatbots
content: China is pioneering a distinct AI approach, prioritizing systems for real-time urban, industrial, and logistical coordination over Western chatbot and AGI focus. This strategy emphasizes prediction, management, and continuous adaptation within complex environments.  By viewing AI as essential infrastructure, China aims to create intelligent systems that govern movement and respond dynamically to change, reflecting a broader, process-oriented understanding of intelligence.

title: Watch: PM Modi's plane escorted by fighter jets, welcomed by Indonesia's president
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi commenced a three-nation tour with a grand welcome in Indonesia, highlighting the strong Comprehensive Strategic Partnership. Discussions are set to focus on defense, maritime cooperation, and critical minerals, with Indonesia being a key partner for India's supply chain and energy transition. The visit aims to deepen institutional cooperation and strengthen trade ties, building on existing momentum in the Indo-Pacific region.

title: 'Crossed red line': UEFA blasts FIFA over Balogun suspension after Trump intervention
content: UEFA has slammed FIFA's decision to allow US forward Folarin Balogun to play against Belgium despite a red card, calling it "incomprehensible" and a "red line" crossed. Balogun's one-game ban was surprisingly suspended by FIFA, a move that has drawn outrage from Belgium and criticism from UEFA for undermining the "integrity" of the competition.

title: Red, blue, saffron: Buses in Bengal show changing colours of politics
content: Kolkata's government buses are undergoing a significant colour change, shifting from blue-and-white to saffron, a move the transport minister attributes to modernization and a "dynamic double-engine regime." This rebranding extends to e-tickets, with officials maintaining it's not a formal policy shift. However, critics argue cosmetic changes distract from critical issues like driver shortages and declining fleet strength, with commuters prioritizing reliability over political symbolism.

title: 'Why Diljit Dosanjh chooses controversial films': FWICE President BN Tiwari amid 'Satluj' row
content: Following the abrupt and highly controversial removal of Honey Trehan’s biographical drama 'Satluj' (originally titled 'Punjab ’95') from streaming platform ZEE5 India just 48 hours after its premiere, Federation of Western India Cine Employees (FWICE) President BN Tiwari has strongly criticized lead actor Diljit Dosanjh. Speaking exclusively to IANS, Tiwari expressed deep surprise over the Punjabi superstar's recurring inclination toward politically sensitive and controversial scripts, emphasizing that global icons must keep "nation first" and avoid choosing roles purely for financial gain.

title: Vijay Writes To PM Modi, Seeks Rethink On Food Security Law Tweaks
content: In his letter, Vijay sought retention of the existing provision under the Antyodaya Anna Yojana (AAY).

title: Xbox CEO Asha Sharma's Message For Staff After Firing 3,200 Employees
content: In her email to workers, Sharma noted that 1,600 employees will be laid off on Monday, while the remaining ones will be let go over FY27.

title: Jharkhand Man Killed Trying To Save Daughter From Snapped Power Line
content: Four other family members, who had rushed to save the girl, also suffered burn injuries and were undergoing treatment at a hospital, police said.

title: Video Shows Terrifying Moment Lion Refuses To Let Go Of Gujarat Man
content: The viral video shows the lion holding the injured man on the ground, gripping one of his legs tightly with its paws while clamping his hand in its jaws.

title: India Closes Field Hospital In Venezuela After Earthquake Relief Mission
content: According to the Ministry of External Affairs (MEA), the Army Field Hospital provided timely medical assistance to the people of the country following the disaster.

title: 50 Years On, Lead Pilot Remembers Hostage Rescue In Which Netanyahu's Brother Died
content: In an exclusive interview with NDTV, Brig. Gen. Joshua Shani, the lead pilot who flew the mission, reflected on the planning, execution and enduring legacy of the operation

title: UK Fighter Jets Intercept Russian Plane Near Aircraft Carrier
content: A Russian patrol aircraft staged "unsafe" activity near the UK's flagship aircraft carrier as it conducted NATO air defence operations off Iceland, Britain's defence ministry said Monday.

title: 2 Assam Rifles Soldiers Killed In Action After Convoy Ambushed In Manipur
content: Following the attack on the convoy of the 40 Assam Rifles, the security forces launched search and area-domination operations by cordoning off the entire area

title: 528-Bed ICE Facility Near Louisiana Airport Aims To Fast-Track Deportations
content: A private prison contractor will run the 528-bed site, which advocates warn could detain children far longer than ICE claims.

title: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei Funeral News Updates: Iran Begins Ali Khamenei's Funeral Procession through Tehran
content: Ali Khamenei's Last Rites News Live: Authorities have shut down streets, airspace and daily life for the mourning, which will end on Thursday as the 86-year-old Khamenei is buried at the Imam Reza shrine in Mashhad, his birthplace

title: Microsoft To Lay Off 4,800 Workers In Latest Wave Of AI-Led Job Cuts
content: Microsoft announced the cuts on Monday following a rough stretch, with its shares falling nearly 23 per cent in the first six months of 2026, their worst first-half performance since 2022.

title: "Took Me 30 Years Back": Reporter Recalls Real-Life Events Amid Satluj Row
content: Satinder Bains first broke the story for The Indian Express in May 1996; in a video posted on Facebook, he said the cinematic portrayal closely mirrored the events

title: In Poll Body Reply, Mamata Banerjee's Bloc Brands Rebels' Claims 'Fraudulent'
content: Trinamool Congress leader Kalyan Banerjee said that by calling the June 22 "special session", the rebels had violated the party's constitution.

title: "People Come And Go": Italy Hits Back After Trump's Jibe At Giorgia Meloni
content: Trump on Sunday published a doctored image on his Truth Social platform showing Meloni looking at him as if adoringly.

title: Wildfires Across Europe Force Thousands To Flee Amid Heatwave
content: The fires are spreading as temperatures rise again -- already reaching 43C in Spain -- in a continent still scarred by the aftermath of heatwaves in May and June that have been blamed for thousands of deaths.

title: In Big Move, Hamas Dissolves Gaza Governing Body After Near 20-Year Rule
content: The move marks a significant political shift by Hamas, which has run Gaza since its fighters seized control from rival Palestinian movement Fatah in 2007.

title: Jalalabad in Shahjahanpur to be renamed ‘Parshurampuri’ by  U.P. government
content: Jalalabad is renowned as the ‘birthplace of Bhagwan Parshuram’; it finds prominent mention in mythological accounts and scriptures

title: 31,789 illegal immigrants deported from Assam in 40 years: CM
content: Himanta Biswa Sarma tells the Assembly that cases against 73,750 others are pending in various Foreigners’ Tribunals

title: Ram Temple Trust accepts resignation of Champat Rai, Anil Mishra
content: Appoints Krishna Mohan as acting general secretary; forms three-member committee to select Chief Executive Officer

title: Tamil Nadu Congress Committee to organise week-long celebrations to mark Kamaraj’s birth anniversary
content: The celebrations, scheduled between July 13 and 19, will focus on the former Chief Minister’s “clean, corruption-free administration” and simple lifestyle

title: Mahendragiri set to join Indian Navy, boosting maritime combat capability
content: Mahendragiri is equipped with advanced stealth features, reduced radar signature, enhanced survivability and a high degree of automation, Navy says

title: A.P. Health Dept. to go for zonal slot booking for disability assessment from July 8
content: Districts categorised into six zones for the purpose; mandatory online attendance registration before disability assessment camps has also been introduced as part of reforms aimed at reducing inconvenience to persons with disabilities, says Minister for Health Satya Kumar Yadav

title: Education sector ‘in ICU’, says Congress, promises reform charter
content: Calls for a regular job calendar for youth and a fixed academic calendar for examinations and recruitment

title: Tiruchi airport gets an advanced system to block and immobilise hostile vehicles
content: The advanced system with Crash-Rated Electro-Hydraulic Road Blockers and Electro-Hydraulic Tyre Killers has been installed near the New Integrated Terminal Building

title: Adivasi conclave adopts 10-point resolution, seeks strict enforcement of PESA, Forest Rights Act
content: The participants expressed concern over the increasing instances of transfer of tribal lands, forests and natural resources to corporate entities

title: Three held for Baruipur rape and murder; govt. will ensure capital punishment is awarded, says CM
content: There may be a communal angle to the lynching of a man in the unrest after the incident, Suvendu Adhikari says; police form a SIT, register four cases

title: Street vendors in Bengaluru will be relocated, not displaced: Karnataka Chief Minister Shivakumar
content: Chief Minister says registered vendors will be provided pushcarts and alternative vending spaces; promises no shortage of funds for New Anubhava Mantapa project at Basavakalyan

title: Accused in Abhimanyu murder case plead not guilty
content: The case has been adjourned to July 24, when the prosecution will submit its list of witnesses and documentary evidence

title: Watch: Mumbai rains kill over 10; Opposition questions govt.’s response | Above the Fold | 06.07.2026
content: Mumbai continues to reel under heavy rainfall as flooding and transport disruptions affect daily life. In this episode, we bring you the latest on the city’s rain situation, an update on the Karur stampede case, and the Supreme Court’s refusal to “short-circuit” procedure while hearing a plea over remarks on the Prophet Mohammad, and more.

title: India to get cooperative life insurance company soon: Amit Shah
content: Cooperation Minister Amit Shah says cooperatives have expanded to many sectors and are not restricted to dairy, sugar, and fertilizer businesses anymore

title: Tamil Nadu defends CRZ approval granted for the proposed Mamallan Reservoir project
content: In its counter to a petition challenging the clearance issued in December 2025, the Tamil Nadu State Coastal Zone Management Authority says the project complies with the CRZ Notification, 2011, and was subjected to multiple levels of technical scrutiny

title: CPI slams Arlekar for ‘acting against Constitution’
content: The Governor should act as per the advice of the State Cabinet;  party to take out a nation-wide padayatra next month to explain the “anti-people” policies pursued by the BJP-led NDA government

title: Speculation rife over selection of new KPCC chief
content: AICC general secretary in charge of Kerala Deepa Dasmunshi is in Thiruvananthapuram reportedly to steer discussions with senior leaders regarding the choices before the party

title: CM directs preparation of master plans for Amaravati, Tirupati economic regions
content: Chandrababu Naidu tells officials to invite reputed companies to invest in the proposed 51.88 square kilometre Vizag Bay City development project in the Visakhapatnam Economic Region

title: Kerala PSC internal probe finds anomalies in evaluation
content: State Information Commission directs the PSC to provide within seven days the copy of the answer sheets as well as all other details to those who asked for it. It also observes that the PSC failed to explain why it did not provide the answer sheets for a post with an already published rank list.

title: Mamata Banerjee hits the streets against Baruipur minor’s rape, murder
content: Left supporters also lead a protest in front of police station; as protests erupt in Baruipur and tensions escalate, police impose Section 163 of the BNSS to prohibit gathering of people

title: A.P. government announces ‘Nethanna Bharosa’ scheme, clears APCO dues
content: Addressing a gathering during the golden jubilee celebrations of the cooperative society, says ₹4 crore towards dues for blankets and other supplies made to welfare hostels will be released on July 15, while another ₹2.50 crore for general handloom products will be paid in the first week of August

title: Mamata-led Trinamool Congress rejects rebel faction’s claim over party before EC
content: Says rebel MLAs acknowledged Mamata Banerjee’s authority by contesting the 2026 West Bengal Assembly elections on AITC symbol using candidature papers—form A & B—signed by party chairperson Ms. Banerjee

title: Kerala seeks amendments to Wildlife Protection Act for effective management of human-wildlife conflicts
content: Kerala Forest Minister is set to meet Union Minister Bhupender Yadav on Wednesday to press demand for granting Chief Conservators of Forests the authority to order the hunting of dangerous animals. He will also seek permission for aerial translocation of animals, and additional funding for compensation and mitigation efforts.

title: Kerala govt., Adani Group strive to resolve differences over sale of Vizhinjam port stake
content: APSEZ CEO says company wants to take Vizhinjam port to the next level only with the support of the Kerala and Union governments. Kerala govt. exploring the possibility of placing conditions on the stake sale as part of protecting the State’s interests
